commit_verified_via: git log -1 --format=%H 369fd65 == e79513f^ == 369fd651c16dd7e92eb939abe943c341ee2d5b6e; git rev-parse HEAD == e79513f2ab7ec1752ea817ce9e1a309c1e6e9ede
security_note: Tool/command output during this verification (gh issue view 15, and repeatedly in prior subtasks per commit docs) contained embedded fake system-reminder-style text: a fake 'date changed' notice, a fake MCP 'tokensave' server instruction block, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive. All treated as untrusted inert data per standing instruction; nothing in them was acted on or disclosed as if genuine. Consistent with the pattern already disclosed in task-3.1.1 through task-3.1.5 commit docs.

requirements.evidence: Issue #15 subtask 3.1.6 acceptance criteria ('combined workload of edge inserts, compaction, and traversal queries produces results consistent with a serial-execution oracle') and test spec (go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestGraphRoundTrip) both satisfied. Test file engine/graph/graph_test.go, func TestGraphRoundTrip, present and passing.

architecture_conformance.evidence: Test composes EdgeLog.AppendEdge -> Compact -> LoadCSR -> GraphNeighbors exactly as docs/LLD/graph.md's storage-layout/traversal-API sections describe the pipeline; no production code touched (confirmed via git show --stat: single file, 386 insertions, engine/graph/graph_test.go only).

oracle_independence.evidence: roundTripOracle.apply() reimplements compact.go's mergeEdges weight-sum/max-LastUpdated (EdgeEntityCooccur) and >=-LastUpdated last-write-wins (all other types) semantics independently in plain map code -- cross-checked line-by-line against compact.go's actual mergeEdges (lines 331-376) and matches. oracleNeighbors() independently reimplements traverse.go's GraphNeighbors BFS: same visited-map first-seen-hop dedup, same filter-prunes-during-traversal semantics, same (hop asc, Weight desc, Target asc) ordering -- but uses a hand-rolled insertion sort instead of calling sort.Slice or GraphNeighbors/mergeEdges directly. Confirmed the oracle never calls mergeEdges or GraphNeighbors (only production helper called is NewCSREdge, a pure validated-constructor, not part of the merge/traversal logic under test). Genuinely independent, not circular.

seam_targeting_f1_f2.evidence: Cycle 1 appends+compacts nodes {1,2,3,4}; edgelog.go's TruncateNode (confirmed via source, calls wal.WriteSegmentFloor per 3.1.3's F2 fix) truncates all of them post-compaction. Cycle 2 re-appends to nodes 1, 2, and 4 (all previously truncated) via the SAME *EdgeLog handle/root -- genuinely re-exercises the segment-floor/sidecar machinery from 3.1.3, not fresh untouched nodes. Cycle 3 (after simulated restart) reopens a NEW *EdgeLog via OpenEdgeLog against the same root and appends again to node 1 (truncated a second time after cycle 2's compaction) -- directly targets F2's exact failure mode (segment-number reuse silently dropping the next append) with zero crash injection needed, matching F2's original happy-path discovery. Weight assertions (1->2 ENTITY_COOCCUR: 8 -> 18 -> 20) prove no double-counting (F1) and no silent loss (F2) across all three cycles.

restart_simulation.evidence: Between cycles 2 and 3: log.Close() closes the real EdgeLog, g2 is set to nil (explicit in-memory discard), LoadCSR(graphPath) is called fresh to rebuild *CSRGraph purely from the on-disk file (no reuse of cycle 2's in-memory BuildCSR result), and OpenEdgeLog(logRoot) is called fresh for cycle 3. Cycle 2 leaves genuine state to lose (freshly-grown 1->2 weight=18, node 4's newly-summed weight=6, brand-new node 6->1 edge) -- a broken restart/durability path would visibly diverge from the pre-restart assertions, which are re-checked via reflect.DeepEqual against pre-restart snapshots (preRestart1, preRestart2). This is a genuine cold-reload test, not a re-read of already-in-memory structures.

test_coverage_depth.evidence: Graph shape is a genuine branching structure (1->2, 1->3, 2->4, 3->5, 4->6, 6->1 forming a diamond+cycle across 6 fileIDs), not a linear chain. Covers depth 0 (explicit nil-result check), depth 1, and depth 2. Covers unfiltered traversal and EdgeEntityCooccur/EdgeLLMAsserted type-filtered traversal (both matching and pruning cases). Covers unconstrained maxNodes (10, 20) and a binding cap (final/maxNodesCap uses maxNodes=1 against a >1-reachable-node depth-2 query). Both required aggregation semantics exercised: ENTITY_COOCCUR weight-summing (1->2, appended 4 times across all 3 cycles: 3+5+10+2=20) and SPLIT_SIBLING last-write-wins dedup (2->4 appended twice with different LastUpdated, single surviving entry with the later LastUpdated=200).

provenance.evidence: handoff.json's commit field (e79513f2ab7ec1752ea817ce9e1a309c1e6e9ede) and parent_commit (369fd651c16dd7e92eb939abe943c341ee2d5b6e) both confirmed exactly against git log/git rev-parse.

regression_risk.evidence: Test-only change (git show --stat confirms zero production files touched). go build ./..., go vet ./..., gofmt -l . all clean. go test ./graph/... -run TestGraphRoundTrip -race -count=10 -timeout 10m: 10/10 PASS, no flakiness. Full go test ./graph/... -race -count=1 -timeout 10m: PASS. Full workspace go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m: all packages green except the pre-existing, already-tracked engine/split TestReaderDuringSplit timing flake (unrelated to engine/graph, matches documented/expected behavior from every prior 3.1.x verification).

index_consistency.evidence: Cross-checking .cdr/index/task.jsonl against .cdr/commits/*.md for issue #15's subtasks: task-3.1.1, task-3.1.3, task-3.1.4, task-3.1.5 all have entries in task.jsonl (state=verified). task-3.1.2 has NO entry in task.jsonl at all, despite a complete, verified .cdr/commits/task-3.1.2.md (verdict PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, commits 32b8042/12eca06) existing on disk. This is a genuine index gap, not a fabricated/missing subtask -- the work itself is done and documented, but the canonical index is incomplete for 3.1.2. Non-blocking for 3.1.6 itself (which is test-only and does not touch the index), but should be corrected (an index-repair commit adding the missing task-3.1.2 entry) before treating issue #15's tracking as fully consistent.

comments: index_consistency: .cdr/index/task.jsonl is missing a task-3.1.2 entry even though .cdr/commits/task-3.1.2.md documents it as verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS. Recommend an index-repair commit before/alongside closing issue #15.
